hypothesis: If diffusion-based generative models trained on curated MP and OQMD datasets produce chemically plausible crystal structures, then the DFT-integrated evaluation framework will (relative to controlled perturbations) assign those generated structures significantly lower post-relaxation RMSD, lower energy above the convex hull (SUN), and closer formation-energy and density distributions (lower EMD) with higher latent precision/recall, because the combined simulation-based, property-based, and latent-based metrics capture geometric stability, thermodynamic realism, and distributional fidelity.
